agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being specified for customer-facing delivery visibility. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements regarding whether courier GPS location should be exposed to customers. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved at the engineering level — it requires executive alignment before any implementation begins.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Order Fulfillment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver employed or contracted by the company to fulfill last-mile delivery of packages to customers."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The current GPS coordinates of a courier, updated at a frequent interval (typically every few seconds), as reported by the courier's mobile device."},{"term":"Delivery tracking","definition":"The customer-facing capability to monitor the progress of their order from dispatch to delivery completion."},{"term":"Customer transparency","definition":"The degree to which order and delivery status information is shared with the end customer."},{"term":"Courier privacy","definition":"The protection of courier personal data, including precise geolocation, from exposure to parties who do not operationally require it."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary at a personal location (e.g., bathroom break, lunch) and customer sees exact coordinates","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — depends on resolution of the core contradiction. Exact GPS would expose personal stops; abstracted progress would not."},{"scenario":"Courier deviates from expected route (e.g., multi-drop optimization, road closure)","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — exact GPS shows deviation and may cause customer anxiety; no location hides useful progress info."},{"scenario":"Courier is between deliveries in a batch and customer sees them moving away","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — exact GPS reveals multi-stop routing to other customers; abstracted status avoids this confusion."},{"scenario":"Regulatory jurisdiction requires or prohibits worker location sharing (e.g., GDPR, state privacy laws)","expected_behavior":"Cannot define without legal review — compliance requirements may override both teams' preferences."},{"scenario":"Courier opts out of location sharing but customer demands visibility","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — directly depends on which stakeholder requirement takes precedence."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High risk in both directions. Customer Success claims transparency drives acquisition/retention (revenue). Operations claims courier attrition is a cost center — replacing a courier costs $3K-$5K in recruiting, onboarding, and lost capacity. Both have legitimate financial arguments that cannot be weighed without data.","operational":"Showing exact GPS creates operational burden: couriers modify behavior when watched (Hawthorne effect), support tickets increase when customers see 'wrong' movements, and driver retention drops. NOT showing location may increase 'where is my order' inbound contacts. Both outcomes have real ops cost.","user":"Two user groups in direct conflict. Customers want maximum visibility. Couriers — who are also users of the platform — want privacy and autonomy. Optimizing for one degrades the other.","risk":"CRITICAL: Building to either spec without executive resolution risks (a) shipping a feature that gets reversed, wasting engineering effort, or (b) accelerating courier churn, or (c) losing competitive differentiation. Legal/compliance risk also unassessed — sharing worker GPS may violate labor privacy regulations in some jurisdictions.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Directly affected — their delivery visibility experience depends on resolution. Expectations may already be set by Customer Success messaging."},{"group":"Couriers / Drivers","impact":"Directly affected — their privacy, autonomy, and willingness to remain on the platform is at stake. This is a labor relations issue, not just a feature decision."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Their strategy and customer-facing promises depend on transparency as a differentiator."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Their driver retention metrics and recruitment costs are directly impacted."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Cannot begin implementation without a resolved spec — building to either side risks rework."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Worker location data sharing has regulatory implications (GDPR, CCPA, labor law) that neither team has addressed."},{"group":"Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict — it is a strategic business decision about which side of the marketplace to optimize for."}]},"confidence":{"level":30,"reasoning":"Confidence is critically low because the two authoritative stakeholder teams have issued mutually exclusive requirements, both framed as non-negotiable. There is no spec to implement — there is a business strategy conflict masquerading as a feature request. Additionally, legal/regulatory implications of worker GPS sharing have not been assessed. Until executive arbitration occurs, any acceptance criteria defined would be speculation.","high_confidence_areas":["This is a genuine contradiction that cannot be resolved by engineering","Both stakeholder concerns are legitimate and grounded in real business impact","There are well-known industry patterns (ETA-based tracking, zone-based proximity) that could serve as compromise solutions","Courier privacy has regulatory dimensions that must be assessed"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which stakeholder priority should take precedence","Whether a middle-ground solution (e.g., approximate location, ETA-only, zone-based tracking) would satisfy either team","The actual quantitative impact on courier retention vs customer satisfaction","Regulatory requirements in the company's operating jurisdictions"],"assumptions":["Both teams are acting in good faith and representing real business concerns","No prior executive decision has been made that supersedes either team's position","The company operates in jurisdictions where worker location privacy laws may apply"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates be shown to customers at all times during delivery, calling it a non-negotiable differentiator. Operations requires courier location NEVER be shown to customers, citing driver retention crisis from feeling surveilled. These are mutually exclusive requirements from two authoritative teams. This is not an engineering problem — it is a business strategy conflict that requires executive arbitration.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Customer Success","Operations","Engineering","Legal"],"options":["A: Side with Customer Success — show exact GPS. Risk: accelerated courier churn, possible regulatory exposure.","B: Side with Operations — show no location. Risk: loss of competitive differentiator, customer dissatisfaction.","C: Executive-mediated compromise — implement abstracted tracking (ETA-based, zone-based, or 'stops away') that gives customers useful progress without exposing exact courier coordinates. This is the industry-standard pattern (Uber, DoorDash, Amazon all use approximated or abstracted location, not raw GPS).","D: Courier-consent model — let couriers opt in/out of location sharing, giving them agency. Customers who get a consenting courier see location; others see ETA only."],"recommendation":"Option C. The industry has already solved this tension. No major logistics platform exposes raw GPS to customers. Abstracted tracking (ETA countdown, 'X stops away', neighborhood-level map pin) delivers 90% of the transparency value while protecting courier privacy. Recommend executive working session with both teams to align on an abstracted tracking spec, with legal review of any location-sharing approach before implementation begins."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Mutually exclusive requirements from two authoritative stakeholder teams","business_rule_broken":"Cannot define business rules when the fundamental business policy is unresolved — showing vs. hiding courier location are opposite rules that cannot coexist"},{"violation":"No legal/regulatory assessment of worker GPS data sharing","business_rule_broken":"Worker location data is likely subject to privacy regulations (GDPR Article 88, CCPA, state labor laws) — no compliance review has been performed"},{"violation":"Engineering cannot begin work on a contradicted spec","business_rule_broken":"Implementing either requirement as stated will violate the other team's non-negotiable constraint, guaranteeing rework or organizational conflict"}]}
